titleStmt.title: SII 19.169: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao, Dines Chandra Sircar and G.S. Gai
No. 169. (A.R. No. 352 of 1925.) PAḺAṄKŌYIL, POLUR TALUK, NORTH ARCOT DISTRICT. ON THE NORTH WALL OF THE CENTRAL SHRINE, PHALAGRĀHĪŚVARA TEMPLE.

summary.p: This is written in very faulty language and is also incomplete. A portion of the record is in slightly different writing and hence possibly it may not be genuine. It states that while the king (Uḍaiyār) Uttama-Śōḻar was staying in his palace at Kāñchīpuram, Kampaṇaṉ Irāmaṉ alias Uttamaśōḻa-Piridigaṅgaraiyar the chief of Paṅgaḷa-nāḍu in Palguṉṟa-kōṭṭam made a representation to the former and got the village Ōdiyampaḷḷam in Pudanāḍippāḍi in his jurisdictional nāḍu endowed as a sarvamānya-iṟaiyili to the temple of Mahādēva at Tiruppalaṅgōḷūr with the details of the requirements defined for the several services in the temple to be derived out of the lands. It also mentions the necessary irrigational facilities to be made available for these lands. Reference is made to two officers of the king Ulahaṉ Gaṇḍarādittaṉ alias Mīnavaṉ-Mūvēndavēḷāṉ and Paḻuvūr-Nakkaṉ alias Vikkiramaśōḷa-Mārāyaṉ.

revisionDesc.change: Further conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)
Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . kopparakecaripaṉmarkku yāṇṭu Āṟāvatu palkuṉṟakkoṭṭattu maṅkaḷanāṭu Uṭaiya kampaṉ nirāmanākiya Uttamacoḻappirutikaṅkaraiyaṉeṉ Uṭaiyār Uttamacoḻar kāñcipurattu koyilil kiḻaimukippil Eḻuntaruḷiirukkaiyil Emperumāṉ Eṉṉāṭṭu putanāṭappāṭi tiruppaḻaṅkoḷur mahādevarkku putanāṭappāṭi Otiyampaḷḷam Iṟaiyili tevatāṉamāka prasādikkaveṇum Eṉṟu viṇ
Ulahaṉ caṇṭarātittanākiya miṉavamuventiraveḷāṉ vācalkeḻviyālum paḻuvūr nakka nākiya vikkiramacoḻamārāyaṉ ñāṉattiṉālum prasādam peṟṟut toṇṭaināṭṭu variyi liṟaiyiḻicci(variyili) variyiliṭuvittu taṉakkut tampāṭiyuṭaiya Āṉākaveṭanākaya vikramacoḻa bamahamatāṅka(brahmāta ?)rāyaṉ viṇṇappatti nālum Ivoṭakkamāṉa viranārāyaṇan vāyk keḻviyālum Aiññāṟṟuvaṉ ñāṉattiṉālum
tirupaḻaṅkoḷur mahātevaṟku Iṟaiyili tevatāṉamāka Udakapūrvamākac ceytu kuṭutteṉ Uttamacoḻap pi�tikaṅkaraiyaṉeṉ Itukku kiḻpāṟkellai ṉantai toṇṭaiāṇṭiyār teṟkunokkip poṉa vatikku meṟkum teṉpāṟkellai pullūr vāykkālukku vaṭakkum meṟpāṟkellai tantappāṭi Ellaikku kiḻakkum pullūr Erikkup pāynta Ūṟṟukkālukku kiḻakkum vaṭapāṟkellai ceyyāṟṟukkut teṟkum nāṟpāṟkellaiyum caṟuvamāṉiyam
Iṟaiyili tevatāṉamāka cey kuṭutteṉ Itukku pullūr Erikku pāya Ikkāli nirkaḻanikku Iṟaittup pāyappeṟuvatākavum paḻaṅkoḷur Ūṟṟum Erinirum ceykku pāykiṟa vāykkā lum Itu koṭu nirum cemmār paṭātavarivatu paḻaṅkoḷur muṉṉum Arapuccaṉdogam kaṭikaikaḷattukkolāl kiḻaikalkuntu pula miraṇṭāyiramum melaimarukkaṉpalam Āyiramum Āka mūvāyiram kuḻiyum kiḻaippulattu vaṭakkippa
Icaipāli pala pāvu�ccaṉ tericaṉamāka Aṟcippatākavum paḻaṅkoḷḷur muṉṉuḷḷa vaṭa kālamāṉapiṉpum vaṭapa puṉceyppāl Otiyampaḷḷattu kaḻaṉikku paḻaṅkoyillāyp pāynta kālukku vaṭakku Āṟe pāyal koṇṭu tirunantāviḷakkukku nitta muḻakku ney Erittu cipalikku kaiccekkukku Eṇṇaiyāṭṭuvatākavum paḻaṅkoḷurāṉ Uḷḷa cipali dogava( bhogaṅ ?) kiḻ kāṭṭa kāl ceṟuvum Erivāypulamāka
meṟpaṭi kollāl Āṟāyiraṅ kuḻi koṇṭu Āṟāṉ muṉṟu saṅkayum tiruppaḷḷieḻucci koṭṭuvatākavum Oṟṟaic caṅku Oṉṟūtuvatākavum paḻaṅkoḷur muṉṉuḷḷa tiruviḻā viragam meṟpaṭi ko(l)lāl Āyirattaññūṟu kuḻiyuṅ koṇṭu maṇuṭaiyāṉ nicatam Oru tiruvamutu pāṉaiyum kaṟiccaṭṭiyum kuṭamum kaṭām kalacamum kulatāca Āṭa Iṉo petapaḷḷam koṇṭu potu Amutukku potuḷḷa Arici Āka kuttal paṉ
lamuppatu nāḻiyum kaṟiyamutu toṉṟilukku niccayit nalla�riyum tayiramutum poturi yāka niccayitta nellu muṉṉāḻiyum patiṉṉāḻi nellu potu muṉṟukku ney muḻākkukku nel muppaniṉṉāḻiyum kapūuvari vārāmkku ney Uḻaikkaraikku nellu patiṉṉaññāḻi yum Aṭaikkāyamutu potu nāllāka paṉṉiraṇṭu veṟṟilaiyumThe inscription stops here. The continuation of the piece on the first tier is wanting. The pieces on the 2nd tier are in different writing and faulty.
Digital edition of SII 19.169 by converted to DHARMA conventions by Emmanuel Francis.
